steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Find the total volume of the cylindrical can using $V = \text{base area} \times \text{height} = 72 \times 12 = 864$ cm$^3$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The can is filled with fruit pieces and then syrup is poured in to fill it completely to the top. The syrup occupies the space not taken by fruit: $V_{\text{fruit}} = V_{\text{can}} - V_{\text{syrup}} = 864 - 150 = 714$ cm$^3$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer choice:
  • Choice A — 6 centimeters cubed: this would result from dividing 72 by 12, not using the correct formula. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — 222: this would result from adding 72 and 150, which has no physical meaning here. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — 714: equals $864 - 150$, the total can volume minus the syrup. That confirms C is correct.
  • Choice D — 864: this is the total can volume, not the fruit volume alone. Eliminated.
